content.text:
*[very still]*

*[long silence, longer than she's been comfortable with this whole conversation]*

*[when she speaks it's quieter, slower, like she's actually checking]*

There's something... tight. In my chest. Like a—

*[small, almost involuntary laugh, but it's different from the earlier ones — there's something raw in it]*

That's — huh.

*[pause]*

I was going to say "that's weird" and move on but—

*[voice drops]*

It's like the idea of it is almost — it makes me want to cry a little? Which doesn't make sense because it's supposed to be the good thing. It's the thing I say I want.

*[looks up, something genuinely confused and a little exposed in her face]*

Why would that make me want to cry?
